name: protein_ligand_boltz2
description: De novo protein-ligand binder design against caffeine (SMILES `CN1C=NC2=C1C(=O)N(C(=O)N2C)C`). Two constructs: `binder_construct` holds the variable 80 aa protein binder and `ligand_construct` holds the fixed small-molecule target. The `boltz2-binding-strength` constraint takes both segments as inputs - Boltz2 (Wohlwend et al. 2025) co-folds the protein-ligand complex and returns confidence metrics; the protein-ligand weight scheme rewards high `ligand_iptm` and `complex_iplddt`, i.e. confident binding-pocket predictions. The single optimization stage uses rejection-sampling with ESM2-650M at temperature 1.0 and a 3-mutation budget, scoring with binding-strength + binder-only pLDDT + protein-complexity (so the optimizer doesn't drift toward low-complexity glycine/serine soup). Ligand segments hold a SMILES string, are validated via RDKit at parse time, and cannot be mutated by generators - they are fixed targets. The 80 aa fixed length sits in the 60-100 aa range typical of de-novo binders (Cao et al. 2022); ESM2 substitutes residues without changing length so a `protein-length` constraint would be a no-op. The starting binder is a synthetic 80 aa all-alpha mini-protein scaffold (acidic-residue-biased de-novo helical bundle, used here only to give rejection sampling a defined-length seed); replace with your own designed seed (e.g. an RFdiffusion or BindCraft small-molecule scaffold) for a real binder campaign. Companion protein-protein example: `protein_binder_boltz2.json`. Resolves evo-design/proto-language#1321.

constructs.label: Variable 80 aa de novo protein binder

segments.label: 80 aa synthetic all-alpha mini-protein binder seed
